text: Adapter experiments should measure held-out domain facts separately from general retention. The evaluation record is intentionally distinct from the smoke training note.
Domain-adaptive pretraining adjusts a base model toward a target corpus while the lab tracks how much general capability is traded away in the process.
A noise floor is the smallest metric movement that repeated evaluation and bootstrap resampling cannot distinguish from measurement noise.
LoRA adapters keep the base weights frozen, so disabling the adapter recovers the original model behaviour exactly.
Partial unfreezing trains a chosen subset of base weights and cannot be reverted by switching an adapter off.
A replay buffer mixes general documents back into the domain token stream at a configured ratio to slow catastrophic forgetting.
Contamination checks compare training documents against registered evaluation examples before the corpus is packed into token blocks.
Fixed-length packing concatenates documents into uniform blocks, inserting an end-of-sequence token between documents so the model learns where examples stop.
Forgetting detection compares checkpoint perplexity against a calibrated noise floor rather than reacting to any raw increase in general loss.
A matched token budget gives two training strategies the same number of effective tokens so that a comparison reflects the method and not the data seen.
Reproducibility manifests record git commit, library versions, data hashes, and seeds so a reported result can be regenerated from the same inputs.
Checkpoint resume must restore optimizer moments, scheduler state, and the random number generator, not only the trained weights.
